metadata.title: grown-workspace - Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M)
metadata.remarks: Honest gap analysis. Each poam-item references a risk and the affected 800-53 control(s). Severities are the author's assessment for a Moderate baseline. UNVALIDATED against an OSCAL validator. Milestone dates are illustrative placeholders. The system is NOT authorized; these are the gaps that would block authorization.

observations.title: MFA capable but not enforced
observations.description: TOTP and passkey/FIDO2 enrollment are exposed via internal/zitadelproxy, but no Zitadel login policy enforces MFA, so an org member can authenticate with a single factor.

observations.title: No FIPS 140-validated cryptographic module
observations.description: AES-256-GCM (pdf/backend/internal/crypto/keystore.go) and TLS rely on Go standard-library crypto and Let's Encrypt; none is operated in a FIPS 140-2/3 validated mode.

observations.title: No continuous monitoring / vuln scanning / SIEM
observations.description: Audit data is reviewed only on-demand via the admin viewer; there is no vulnerability scanning, log forwarding, alerting, or SIEM integration.

observations.title: No application-level encryption at rest / no attestation
observations.description: Neither Postgres rows nor blob payloads are encrypted by grown; at-rest protection depends on unattested underlying-disk encryption. (Exception: the PDF KEK encrypts signing private keys only.)

observations.title: No audit retention / forwarding policy; append-only not enforced at DB
observations.description: audit_events grows unbounded in Postgres with no retention period, archival, or off-host forwarding. Append-only is enforced only by the absence of mutating code paths, not by a DB constraint or a revoked DELETE grant.

observations.title: No IR / contingency-plan / boundary-protection documentation
observations.description: There is no documented incident-response plan, contingency/DR plan tied to grown, or boundary-protection (firewall/NetworkPolicy/WAF) documentation for the grown workload.

observations.title: No role/RBAC model; admin authority is an env allowlist with open fallback
observations.description: grown has no role/owner concept (internal/users, internal/orgs). Admin authority is conferred by GROWN_ADMIN_EMAILS; when empty, privileged writes fall open to any authenticated member (internal/admin/service.go requireAdmin TODO).

observations.title: Multi-tenancy is single-org in practice
observations.description: Although every table is org_id-scoped, multi-org request routing is stubbed (internal/tenancy/middleware.go); the system runs single-org with a default org, so cross-tenant isolation is untested at runtime.

observations.title: No 800-171 SSP / SPRS submission
observations.description: No NIST SP 800-171 self-assessment, System Security Plan of record, or SPRS score has been produced or submitted; CMMC L2 prerequisites are unmet.

observations.title: No SAST / dependency scan / SBOM gate in the grown pipeline
observations.description: The grown build is reproducible and minimal but enforces no static-analysis, dependency-vulnerability, or SBOM-generation gate.

risks.title: Single-factor authentication permitted
risks.description: Without enforced MFA, a compromised password grants full account access, defeating much of the IA/AC posture.

risks.title: Non-FIPS-validated cryptography
risks.description: FedRAMP/CMMC require FIPS 140-validated modules for protecting CUI; current crypto would fail SC-13 / 800-171 3.13.11 assessment.

risks.title: No continuous monitoring or alerting
risks.description: Threats and vulnerabilities can go undetected; no automated detection of anomalous audit patterns.

risks.title: Unattested encryption at rest
risks.description: Data confidentiality at rest cannot be asserted; SC-28 / 800-171 3.13.16 unmet absent attestation or app-level encryption.

risks.title: Audit integrity & retention gap
risks.description: Audit records could be deleted by anyone with DB write access, and unbounded growth risks capacity exhaustion; AU-4/AU-9/AU-11 partially unmet.

risks.title: No incident-response / contingency / boundary documentation
risks.description: IR-*/CP-*/SC-7 require documented, tested plans and boundary protections; none exist for grown.

risks.title: Coarse, open-fallback authorization
risks.description: Lack of a real RBAC model and the open-when-empty admin fallback risk privilege escalation; AC-6/AC-3 weakened.

risks.title: Untested cross-tenant isolation
risks.description: org_id scoping is pervasive but multi-org routing is stubbed, so isolation is unexercised at runtime; a routing bug could expose cross-tenant data.

risks.title: No 800-171 self-assessment / SPRS score
risks.description: CMMC L2 cannot proceed without a documented 800-171 SSP and SPRS submission.

risks.title: No supply-chain scanning gate
risks.description: Vulnerable dependencies or insecure code patterns could ship undetected; RA-5/SA-11/SA-15 partially unmet.

poam-items.title: POAM-001: Enforce MFA org-wide
poam-items.description: Enable a Zitadel login policy that requires a second factor (TOTP or passkey) for all users; optionally block password-only sessions in grown. Affected: AC-2(1), IA-2(1)(2) / 800-171 3.5.3.

poam-items.title: POAM-002: Adopt FIPS 140-validated cryptography
poam-items.description: Build with a FIPS-validated crypto provider (e.g. Go BoringCrypto / a validated module) for TLS and the PDF KEK; document module certificate numbers. Affected: SC-13, SC-12, SC-8 / 800-171 3.13.11.

poam-items.title: POAM-003: Stand up continuous monitoring, vuln scanning, and SIEM
poam-items.description: Forward audit_events and infra logs to a SIEM; add anomaly alerting; run scheduled vulnerability scans on the image and cluster. Affected: CA-7, RA-5, SI-4, AU-6(1).

poam-items.title: POAM-004: Establish & attest encryption at rest
poam-items.description: Enable and ATTEST volume/database encryption for Postgres and the blob store (or add app-level encryption); document key management. Affected: SC-28, SC-28(1) / 800-171 3.13.16.

poam-items.title: POAM-005: Audit retention, forwarding & DB-enforced immutability
poam-items.description: Define an audit retention period; forward audit_events off-host; enforce append-only at the DB (revoke UPDATE/DELETE from the app role, or use a write-only role / trigger). Affected: AU-4, AU-9, AU-11 / 800-171 3.3.4, 3.3.8.

poam-items.title: POAM-006: Author IR, contingency, and boundary-protection docs
poam-items.description: Write and exercise an incident-response plan and a contingency/DR plan for grown; document boundary protection (NetworkPolicies, firewall, WAF/rate-limiting). Affected: IR-1/IR-4/IR-8, CP-1/CP-2/CP-9, SC-7.

poam-items.title: POAM-007: Introduce a real role/RBAC model
poam-items.description: Add org membership roles (owner/admin/member) and require an admin role for privileged operations; remove the open-when-empty allowlist fallback. Affected: AC-2, AC-3, AC-6, AC-6(1)(2) / 800-171 3.1.5.

poam-items.title: POAM-008: Exercise and test multi-tenant isolation
poam-items.description: Implement multi-org request routing (internal/tenancy) and add cross-tenant isolation tests; consider Postgres row-level security as defense-in-depth. Affected: AC-3, AC-4, SC-4.

poam-items.title: POAM-009: Produce 800-171 SSP & SPRS submission
poam-items.description: Complete a NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 self-assessment, author the SSP of record, compute the SPRS score, and submit. Prerequisite for CMMC L2.

poam-items.title: POAM-010: Add supply-chain scanning to CI
poam-items.description: Gate the build on SAST, dependency-vulnerability scanning, and SBOM generation (e.g. govulncheck, Trivy, Syft). Affected: RA-5, SA-11, SA-15.
